YOLO vs Faster R-CNN Under Limited Compute¶

Presentation Notebook¶

Agenda¶

  1. Problem framing and constraints
  2. Data and evaluation protocol
  3. Baseline ablations (10 epochs)
  4. Hero extension (30 epochs total)
  5. Key findings and takeaways
In [1]:
from pathlib import Path
import json
import pandas as pd
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
from PIL import Image
from IPython.display import display, Markdown

ROOT = Path.cwd()
RUNS = ROOT / 'runs'
QH = RUNS / 'qualitative_hero'

paths = {
    'summary_csv': RUNS / 'summary_results.csv',
    'baseline_plot': RUNS / 'comparison_plots.png',
    'hero_metrics_csv': QH / 'hero_test_eval_metrics.csv',
    'hero_deltas_csv': QH / 'hero_test_eval_deltas.csv',
    'hero_split_json': QH / 'hero_test_split_info.json',
    'hero_delta_plot': QH / 'hero_test_ap_deltas.png',
    'hero_ap_plot': QH / 'hero_test_before_after_ap.png',
    'hero_ap50_plot': QH / 'hero_test_before_after_ap50.png',
    'hero_ap75_plot': QH / 'hero_test_before_after_ap75.png',
    'yolo_results_plot': RUNS / 'detect' / 'runs' / 'yolo_resnet50_sgd_hero' / 'results.png',
}

print('Project root:', ROOT)
print('Artifact check:')
for k, p in paths.items():
    print(f'  {k}:', 'OK' if p.exists() else f'MISSING -> {p}')

Project root: c:\Users\Magz8\OneDrive\Documents\GitHub\COCO Object Detection
Artifact check:
  summary_csv: OK
  baseline_plot: OK
  hero_metrics_csv: OK
  hero_deltas_csv: OK
  hero_split_json: OK
  hero_delta_plot: OK
  hero_ap_plot: OK
  hero_ap50_plot: OK
  hero_ap75_plot: OK
  yolo_results_plot: OK

Experiment Constraints¶

  • Hardware: NVIDIA RTX 3050 Ti Laptop GPU (4GB VRAM)
  • Data loader workers constrained to 0 for stability
  • Fixed image size: 512
  • Compared families: YOLO and Faster R-CNN
  • Baseline schedule: 10 epochs
  • Hero schedule: continued to 30 epochs total
In [2]:
summary = pd.read_csv(paths['summary_csv'])
summary_ok = summary[summary['status'] == 'ok'].copy()
summary_view = summary_ok[[
    'family', 'backbone', 'optimizer', 'ap', 'ap50', 'ap75', 'train_seconds', 'phase'
]].sort_values(['family', 'backbone', 'optimizer']).reset_index(drop=True)

display(Markdown('### Baseline Results Table (10 Epochs)'))
display(summary_view.style.format({
    'ap': '{:.4f}',
    'ap50': '{:.4f}',
    'ap75': '{:.4f}',
    'train_seconds': '{:.2f}'
}))

Baseline Results Table (10 Epochs)¶

  family backbone optimizer ap ap50 ap75 train_seconds phase
0 fasterrcnn resnet18 sgd 0.0391 0.0830 0.0312 1133.58 sanity-fast
1 fasterrcnn resnet50 adam 0.1084 0.2322 0.0762 1629.68 sanity-fast
2 fasterrcnn resnet50 sgd 0.1189 0.2531 0.0943 1538.13 sanity-fast
3 yolo resnet18 sgd 0.2807 0.4070 0.3004 803.29 sanity-fast
4 yolo resnet50 adam 0.0138 0.0264 0.0132 817.35 sanity-fast
5 yolo resnet50 sgd 0.3639 0.5122 0.3830 812.21 sanity-fast
In [3]:
display(Markdown('### Baseline Summary Figure'))
img = Image.open(paths['baseline_plot'])
plt.figure(figsize=(14, 8))
plt.imshow(img)
plt.axis('off')
plt.title('Short-Schedule Baseline Summary')
plt.show()

Baseline Summary Figure¶

Held-Out Non-Sampled Test Protocol¶

Test set design:

  • Built from non-sampled training pool
  • Seeded random sampling for reproducibility
  • Same test images used for before-vs-after hero comparison
In [4]:
with open(paths['hero_split_json'], 'r', encoding='utf-8') as f:
    split_info = json.load(f)

display(Markdown('### Held-Out Split Details'))
display(pd.DataFrame([split_info]))

Held-Out Split Details¶

seed requested_test_size actual_test_size total_train_images sampled_train_images non_sampled_pool
0 42 500 500 8000 2000 6000
In [5]:
hero_metrics = pd.read_csv(paths['hero_metrics_csv'])
hero_deltas = pd.read_csv(paths['hero_deltas_csv'])

display(Markdown('### Hero Run: Before vs After (Held-Out Test)'))
display(hero_metrics.style.format({'ap': '{:.4f}', 'ap50': '{:.4f}', 'ap75': '{:.4f}'}))

display(Markdown('### Hero Run: Deltas (After - Before)'))
display(hero_deltas.style.format({'delta_ap': '{:+.4f}', 'delta_ap50': '{:+.4f}', 'delta_ap75': '{:+.4f}'}))

Hero Run: Before vs After (Held-Out Test)¶

  model phase ap ap50 ap75
0 YOLO before 0.4607 0.6267 0.4963
1 YOLO after 0.3634 0.5206 0.3894
2 Faster R-CNN before 0.1106 0.2448 0.0798
3 Faster R-CNN after 0.2270 0.4068 0.2345

Hero Run: Deltas (After - Before)¶

  model delta_ap delta_ap50 delta_ap75
0 YOLO -0.0974 -0.1062 -0.1070
1 Faster R-CNN +0.1164 +0.1620 +0.1548
In [6]:
fig, axes = plt.subplots(2, 2, figsize=(16, 10))
plots = [
    ('AP Before/After', paths['hero_ap_plot']),
    ('AP50 Before/After', paths['hero_ap50_plot']),
    ('AP75 Before/After', paths['hero_ap75_plot']),
    ('Delta Summary', paths['hero_delta_plot']),
]

for ax, (title, path) in zip(axes.flat, plots):
    ax.imshow(Image.open(path))
    ax.set_title(title)
    ax.axis('off')

plt.suptitle('Hero Quantitative Visuals', fontsize=16)
plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()

Overfitting Evidence¶

The hero YOLO training curves are useful for diagnosis:

  • Training losses continue improving
  • Validation quality plateaus/oscillates
  • Held-out test AP declines from before to after hero checkpoint
In [7]:
display(Markdown('### YOLO Hero Training Curves'))
img = Image.open(paths['yolo_results_plot'])
plt.figure(figsize=(16, 7))
plt.imshow(img)
plt.axis('off')
plt.title('YOLO Hero: Training/Validation Curves')
plt.show()

YOLO Hero Training Curves¶

In [8]:
panel_dir = QH / 'panels'
panel_files = sorted(panel_dir.glob('*.jpg'))

display(Markdown('### Qualitative Before/After Panels'))

if len(panel_files) == 0:
    print('No panel images found in', panel_dir)
else:
    cols = 2
    rows = (len(panel_files) + cols - 1) // cols
    fig, axes = plt.subplots(rows, cols, figsize=(16, 6 * rows))
    axes = axes.ravel() if hasattr(axes, 'ravel') else [axes]

    for i, p in enumerate(panel_files):
        axes[i].imshow(Image.open(p))
        axes[i].set_title(p.name)
        axes[i].axis('off')

    for j in range(i + 1, len(axes)):
        axes[j].axis('off')

    plt.tight_layout()
    plt.show()

Qualitative Before/After Panels¶

Final Takeaways¶

  1. In short schedules (10 epochs), YOLO clearly outperforms Faster R-CNN in both AP and training efficiency.
  2. Extended training helps Faster R-CNN substantially, showing it was under-trained in the short budget.
  3. YOLO's hero checkpoint underperforms its earlier checkpoint on held-out non-sampled test data.
  4. Ranking remains unchanged after hero: YOLO still leads, but the gap narrows.
